beats.text: A student stands in a forest. Zoom out — it's an ecosystem.
Zoom into the student's eye. An organ. Zoom deeper — a single photoreceptor cell. Zoom deeper still — a mitochondrion, churning out ATP.
Five levels: ecosystem, organism, organ, cell, molecule. Each one nested inside the last.
Now watch — we skip past the top levels and slam all the way down to molecules and cells.
A DNA strand appears. A label reads: 3.5 billion years old. The same genetic code, shared across nearly all life on Earth.
Master this foundation. Everything else — fruit flies, redwoods, you — is just details built on top.
